world.title: Zero lessons earned, was inches away from deal: Iran blames US for talks collapse
world.summary: Iran has accused the US of "maximalism" and shifting goalposts for the collapse of negotiations aimed at finalizing the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU). After 21 hours of discussions, tensions escalated with new threats of blockades. Iran stated that the agreement was "inches away" from completion before the talks ultimately failed to yield any results.

world.title: Iran was inches away from US deal in Islamabad, says Araghchi as Trump announces Hormuz blockade
world.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi stated that Tehran was close to reaching an agreement with the United States during recent talks in Islamabad. This announcement coincided with the US Central Command's declaration of a blockade on all maritime traffic to and from Iranian ports, set to begin at 10 am ET on April 13.

world.title: US announces naval blockade on 'all maritime traffic entering & exiting' Iran ports: What it means
world.summary: The US military will implement a naval blockade on all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian Gulf ports starting April 13 at 1400 GMT. Vessels not destined for Iran will still be permitted to transit the Strait of Hormuz. This measure aims to exert pressure on Iran amid ongoing tensions in the region, impacting shipping routes significantly.

business.title: Jhunjhunwalas-backed IKS healthcare looks to acquire TruBridge for $600 million
business.summary: Inventurus Knowledge Solutions (IKS), supported by the family of late investor Rakesh Jhunjhunwala, is in advanced negotiations to acquire Nasdaq-listed TruBridge for approximately $600 million. This potential acquisition would mark IKS's largest deal to date and enhance its presence in healthcare technology and revenue cycle management in the United States. A formal announcement is anticipated soon.

business.title: Gold falls to near $4,650 as Oil surge, US-Iran tensions hit Fed cuts
business.summary: Gold prices (XAU/USD) opened lower, trading around $4,670 per troy ounce during Asian hours on Monday. The decline is attributed to rising oil prices and heightened tensions between the US and Iran, which are impacting expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. The non-yielding metal faces challenges as inflation risks increase amid the energy price surge.

business.title: MFs cut cash holdings to 16-month low amid March market selloff
business.summary: Mutual fund cash holdings decreased to Rs 1.86 lakh crore in March, marking the lowest level since December 2024. This represents a decline of Rs 24,319 crore, or 12 percent, from Rs 2.1 lakh crore in February. The significant reduction in cash reserves occurred amid a market selloff during March, impacting overall investment strategies.

technology.title: Linux Kernel 7.0 Officially Released, This Is What’s New
technology.summary: Linux kernel 7.0 has been officially released and is available for download. Announced by Linus Torvalds, this version includes enhanced hardware support, improvements to filesystems and networking, and various security enhancements. While it is not considered a major release, it introduces several changes aimed at improving overall performance and compatibility across different systems and devices.

technology.title: Will Forza Horizon 6 Be On Xbox Game Pass?
technology.summary: Forza Horizon 6 is set to release on May 19, 2026, and will be available on Xbox Game Pass for Ultimate and PC subscribers at launch. Players can pre-install the game early. The new installment will feature a racing environment inspired by Japan, showcasing its rich racing culture. Details on subscription tiers for access have been specified.

sports.title: Drama at Wankhede! RCB star Tim David refuses to return ball; umpire annoyed - WATCH
sports.summary: During the IPL 2026 match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Mumbai Indians at Wankhede Stadium, Tim David, an all-rounder for RCB, caused some annoyance to the umpire with a playful act. The incident occurred when David refused to return the ball, leading to a light-hearted moment amidst the competitive atmosphere of the match.

sports.title: IPL 2026: Jasprit Bumrah struggles for breakthroughs, records longest wicketless streak
sports.summary: Jasprit Bumrah is experiencing an unusual performance in IPL 2026, struggling to secure breakthroughs during the Powerplay. Known for his effective yorkers and early wickets, he is currently on the longest wicketless streak of his career. This shift in form has raised concerns among fans and analysts, as Bumrah's effectiveness has been a key aspect of his IPL success.

sports.title: Jannik Sinner Wins Monte Carlo Masters, Becomes World No. 1
sports.summary: Jannik Sinner won his first ATP Masters 1000 title at the Monte Carlo Masters, defeating Carlos Alcaraz. The match took place on April 13, under windy conditions. This victory marks Sinner's first title on clay and guarantees his rise to the top of the ATP rankings, making him the world number one starting Monday.

health.title: Suspicious CCTV camera found installed near Panipat colony
health.summary: The Model Town police removed a suspicious solar-operated CCTV camera from a pole near Mukhija Colony on Jatal road on Sunday. The action followed a tip-off regarding the camera's installation. Authorities are currently gathering information about the individual responsible for placing the camera. The investigation aims to determine the purpose and origin of the device.

health.title: From thalassemia to sickle cell: Bone marrow transplants as a ‘reset’ for genetic blood disorders
health.summary: Bone marrow transplants are gaining traction in India for treating genetic blood disorders such as thalassemia and sickle cell disease, moving beyond their traditional association with cancer. Despite their potential benefits, challenges such as high costs, limited access, and a shortage of donors hinder widespread adoption. This shift in application reflects advancements in medical science and evolving treatment strategies.

health.title: Persistent joint pain in children: It could be hemophilia, not a sports injury
health.summary: Persistent joint pain in children, particularly recurring swelling in the same area, may indicate hemophilia rather than a sports injury. Early diagnosis of this bleeding disorder is crucial to prevent long-term disabilities. Parents should be vigilant if a child avoids weight-bearing activities or shows sudden changes in movement, as these could signal a more serious underlying condition.

science.title: Artemis II: Uniting America Through Space Exploration
science.summary: Artemis II's successful moon mission has inspired Americans and reignited interest in space exploration. The mission showcases the potential for unity through shared goals in science and technology. Concurrently, Russia's Soyuz-5 rocket is reportedly prepared for upcoming space projects, as confirmed by Roscosmos head Dmitry Bakanov to President Vladimir Putin, marking a significant moment in international space endeavors.

science.title: ‘Is it anyone’s job to check for sharks?’ NASA responds after Artemis II splashdown
science.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully splashed down, marking a significant moment in the astronauts' return. The event attracted considerable public interest, with many closely following the mission updates and landing process. Amid the excitement, questions arose regarding safety measures, including whether anyone is responsible for checking for sharks in the splashdown area, highlighting concerns beyond the space mission itself.

science.title: Artemis II: What are 'organ chips' NASA sent to space?
science.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission included an experiment with 'organ chips,' small devices containing lab-grown human tissue. These chips aim to study the effects of deep space on human cells. The mission's objective extends beyond lunar exploration, focusing on understanding how microgravity influences biological processes. This research could provide insights into human health during long-duration space travel.

entertainment.title: Libra Horoscope Today for April 13, 2026: One emotional pattern may need to be named before it gets heavier
entertainment.summary: Libra's horoscope for April 13, 2026, emphasizes the importance of clarity over comfort. Financial stability is linked to balanced choices. A person may seek a clearer answer, and a situation might require firmer terms. Additionally, naming an emotional pattern is advised to prevent it from becoming more burdensome. The day is not characterized as harsh, but rather insightful.

entertainment.title: Cancer Horoscope Today for April 13, 2026: Love asks for sincerity today
entertainment.summary: Cancer's horoscope for April 13, 2026, emphasizes the importance of sincerity in love. As the day progresses, confidence is highlighted as more beneficial than worry. Individuals may find that a specific goal or responsibility requires them to be stronger. Focusing on the present is crucial, as dwelling on past hurts or doubts could hinder new opportunities.

entertainment.title: Box Office: Is Pradeep Ranganathan’s Winning Streak At Risk Due To Love Insurance Kompany?
entertainment.summary: Pradeep Ranganathan’s film, Love Insurance Kompany, had a strong opening at the Indian box office but did not see significant growth on its second day. Ranganathan has quickly become a notable talent in Kollywood, achieving impressive box office success since his acting debut in 2022. The film's performance raises questions about the sustainability of his winning streak.
